{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": [
    "installed-defective"
  ],
  "percent_complete": 70,

  "confidence": 1.0,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Temporary bracing was removed before decking or permanent lateral stability elements were fully installed.",
    "Premature removal of bracing creates an immediate structural collapse hazard under wind or construction loads."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Evacuate the immediate area under and around the unbraced structural steel framing.",
    "Re-install temporary guys, bracing, or struts immediately to stabilize the structure.",
    "Do not proceed with decking or further loading until permanent stability is established and verified."
  ],
  "rationale": "Removing temporary bracing before the structure is secured by decking or permanent lateral-force-resisting systems violates basic safety and erection standards, creating an imminent collapse risk. The work is marked as failed and critical risk despite being physically erected to a rough-complete stage.",
  "references": [
    "AISC 303 Code of Standard Practice (Section 7.10)",
    "OSHA 1926 Subpart R (Steel Erection - 1926.754)"
  ]
}
